skill.name: specdd-adopt
skill.description: Use when Agent needs to add SpecDD to a project or update SpecDD framework files through the CLI before normal SpecDD work begins.
skill.summary: Bootstrap a project with the SpecDD framework via the specdd CLI, creating initial spec files and framework configuration.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 15 external_commands patterns, all of which are false positives from inline code formatting (backticks) in markdown text referencing the specdd CLI tool name. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'system reconnaissance' findings are also false positives, triggered by natural language phrases in the skill description and workflow steps. The skill itself contains no executable code; it is a set of instructions guiding an AI agent to use the specdd CLI to bootstrap project files. No prompt injection attempts were detected.

low_findings.title: CLI Tool Invocation Expected
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to invoke the 'specdd' CLI to bootstrap and update framework files. This is the documented purpose of the skill and is not inherently dangerous, but the agent will execute external commands on the user's system. Users should ensure the specdd CLI is from a trusted source.

content.user_title: Adopt SpecDD Framework in Any Project
content.value_statement: Setting up a new framework manually is error-prone and time-consuming. This skill automates the entire SpecDD adoption process by guiding the agent to check, initialize, and update framework files through the official CLI.
actual_capabilities: Check whether a target repository already has SpecDD bootstrap files or .sdd contracts
Initialize SpecDD framework files in a new project via the specdd CLI
Update existing SpecDD framework files when requested by the operator
Propose the smallest useful initial spec shape for new adoptions
Create .sdd files only when the user explicitly approves the adoption scope
Report adoption status including bootstrap state, CLI results, and remaining setup tasks
limitations: Requires the specdd CLI to be installed and available in the environment
Cannot manually install, edit, or copy framework bootstrap files outside the CLI
Does not modify implementation files unless the user explicitly requests it
Only inspects the repository shape needed for initial boundaries, not deep analysis

use_cases.title: New Project Framework Setup
use_cases.description: Initialize a freshly created repository with the SpecDD framework so the team can begin writing spec-driven development contracts from day one.
use_cases.target_user: Project leads starting a new spec-driven development initiative

use_cases.title: Existing Project Migration
use_cases.description: Add SpecDD to an established codebase that has no existing framework files, creating the initial root project spec before narrower child specs.
use_cases.target_user: Engineering teams adopting SpecDD in legacy or mid-stage projects

use_cases.title: Framework Version Updates
use_cases.description: Update SpecDD framework bootstrap files in a project that already uses SpecDD, triggered by an operator request for a framework upgrade.

prompt_templates.title: Adopt SpecDD in a New Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Adopt SpecDD in the current repository. Check if .specdd/bootstrap.md exists, and if not, use the specdd CLI to initialize the framework files.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting SpecDD adoption in a repository that has no existing framework files

prompt_templates.title: Update Existing SpecDD Framework
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the SpecDD framework files in this project. The bootstrap files already exist; run the specdd CLI update command to bring them to the latest version.
prompt_templates.scenario: Refreshing framework files in an already-bootstrapped SpecDD project

prompt_templates.title: Propose Initial Spec Shape
prompt_templates.prompt: After initializing SpecDD, inspect the repo and propose a minimal root project spec. Do not create child specs yet.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating the first .sdd file after framework bootstrap is complete

prompt_templates.title: Report Adoption Status
prompt_templates.prompt: Report the adoption status: whether the repo was already bootstrapped, CLI command results, the proposed scope, and any remaining setup tasks for the user.
prompt_templates.scenario: Summarizing the adoption workflow outcome to the operator

output_examples.input: Adopt SpecDD in this new repository.
output: Proposed scope: root project spec covering the main application module.
No .sdd files created yet — awaiting your approval.

output_examples.input: Update the SpecDD framework in this existing project.
output: Framework files updated to latest version.
Bootstrap chain read; no spec changes needed unless you request them.

best_practices: Always check for existing .specdd/bootstrap.md before running init commands to avoid overwriting framework files
Use the specdd CLI for all framework file changes; never edit bootstrap files manually
Propose a minimal root spec first, then expand to child specs only after user approval
anti_patterns: Manually creating or editing .specdd/bootstrap.md files outside the CLI workflow
Creating .sdd spec files without explicit user request or approval
Switching to implementation work or modifying source code during the adoption phase

faq.question: What does the specdd-adopt skill do?
faq.answer: It guides the agent to add SpecDD to a project or update its framework files using the specdd CLI, before normal spec authoring work begins.

faq.question: When should I use this skill versus specdd-author?
faq.answer: Use specdd-adopt for initial framework setup or operator-requested framework updates. Use specdd-author for creating or editing .sdd spec files.

faq.question: Does this skill require the specdd CLI?
faq.answer: Yes. The specdd CLI must be installed and available. If it is not, the skill reports that setup is blocked until the CLI is available.

faq.question: Will this skill modify my implementation files?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly avoids changing implementation files unless the user explicitly requests implementation work.

faq.question: What if my project already has SpecDD?
faq.answer: The skill detects existing .specdd/bootstrap.md and switches to an update workflow or hands off to specdd-author for normal spec work.

faq.question: Is the specdd CLI safe to use?
faq.answer: The skill delegates all framework file changes to the official specdd CLI. Users should ensure the CLI comes from a trusted source such as the SpecDD project repository.
